decision_summary: Approve the first v7.0 Agent Session Observability implementation slice.
rationale: The slice converts AI work from an opaque conversation into a reconstructable event stream linked to task, requirement, test evidence, risk candidate, decision candidate, and release-ready evidence. This directly addresses the v7 direction that AI work should be auditable before it becomes release evidence.
recommendation: Keep the first implementation narrow: local file-backed agent-session-record artifacts, session-observability-audit, CLI registry exposure, and focused regression tests. Do not add vendor streaming APIs or Mission Control projection until this local contract proves stable.
target_audience: AOF operator and maintainer evaluating v7.0 readiness.
expected_user_reaction: The operator should be able to see what the AI did, what task it advanced, what requirement it served, what evidence tested it, what risks and decisions emerged, and why it can or cannot support release readiness.
artifact_change_recommendations: Keep agent-session-record as the canonical local event stream artifact.
Keep session-observability-audit as a narrow structural/runtime gate before Mission Control projection.
Add Mission Control projection only after multiple real streams exist.
organization_change_recommendations: Treat risk candidates and decision candidates emitted by AI sessions as review inputs, not final decisions.
Require session-observability-audit evidence before using AI session work as release-ready support.
